intents.description: Claim awaiting_documentation. Returns evidence inline.
intents.name: claim_doc_task

intents.description: Claim an assembled-PR review task (awaiting_pr_review) WITHOUT transitioning it — mirrors QA's claim_review. The assembled diff and the parent task's acceptance criteria are returned inline.
intents.name: claim_gate_review

intents.description: Claim an inbound external-PR review task and start work. pending -> claimed -> in_progress.
intents.name: claim_pr_review

intents.description: Claim a task in awaiting_qa for review. Returns evidence inline.
intents.name: claim_review

intents.description: Cell PM merges the PR (leaf into the cell branch, or the gated cell→root PR into the root branch) + transitions to completed; Main PM escalates the root to the CEO (who merges root→master). The merge runs BEFORE the complete transition: TaskService.complete asserts the PR is already merged, so the choreographer verb body (cell_pm_complete / main_pm_complete) owns the merge-first ordering — no trailing pr_merge side_effect is declared here.
intents.name: complete

intents.description: Create a subtask under the current task. Validates the delegation chain (main_pm->cell_pm; cell_pm->its team's devs) and the assignee-vs-task_type rule (Cell PMs get planning-typed tasks; devs get code/research, UX devs also design). documentation is NOT delegatable — the lifecycle auto-creates the doc phase after the code subtask passes QA.
intents.name: delegate

intents.description: Escalate to CEO with reason. Transitions to awaiting_ceo_approval.
intents.name: escalate_to_ceo

intents.description: Escalate to your role's escalation_target.
intents.name: escalate_up

intents.description: Fail QA with concrete issues. Transitions to needs_revision.
intents.name: fail_review

intents.description: Return your most-actionable task or signal idle.
intents.name: give_me_work

intents.description: Escalate to PM. Logs a struggle journal entry.
intents.name: i_am_blocked

intents.description: Submit work for QA. Auto-runs in_progress->verifying then verifying->awaiting_qa. Strict - PR must be open (call open_pr first) and >=1 commit.
intents.name: i_am_done

intents.description: Signal you have no active work. PMs auto-pause owned in_progress tasks.
intents.name: i_am_idle

intents.description: Signal docs complete. Transitions to awaiting_pm_review.
intents.name: i_documented

intents.description: PM mirror of i_will_work_on for parent tasks. Claim, plan, transition to in_progress; from there delegate subtasks.
intents.name: i_will_plan

intents.description: Claim a task, set the plan, and transition to in_progress. Atomic - preconditions checked before any state mutation.
intents.name: i_will_work_on

intents.description: Push the branch and open a PR. Atomic - preconditions (assignee, >=1 commit, no prior PR) checked BEFORE any git operation. After success, call i_am_done.
intents.name: open_pr

intents.description: Pass QA. Transitions awaiting_qa -> awaiting_documentation.
intents.name: pass_review

intents.description: Post one complete change-request to the external PR and finish the review task. in_progress -> completed.
intents.name: post_pr_review

intents.description: Fail the assembled-PR review with concrete issues. Transitions awaiting_pr_review -> needs_revision, routed back like a QA fail.
intents.name: pr_fail

intents.description: Pass the assembled-PR review. Transitions awaiting_pr_review -> awaiting_pm_review so the PM can merge.
intents.name: pr_pass

intents.description: Hand a claimed/in_progress task to another developer in your own cell. The branch is keyed to the task (not the agent), so it is preserved — the new developer continues the work-in-progress. No status change.
intents.name: reassign

intents.description: Reject the merge review with concrete issues. Transitions awaiting_pm_review -> needs_revision, routed back like a QA fail (original developer for a leaf, revision PM for an assembled task). Use this for an AC/scope violation caught at merge review — never i_am_blocked/escalate, which have no revision routing.
intents.name: request_changes

intents.description: Resume a paused task you own. paused -> in_progress.
intents.name: resume

intents.description: Main PM opens the root→master PR and moves the root task to awaiting_pr_review for the main reviewer (the root analogue of the cell PM's submit_up). After pr_pass, call complete to escalate to the CEO. For branch-bearing roots (a Main-PM root-subtask assembles the cells' merged work); branchless coordination roots skip the gate and complete directly. The gate is branch-keyed, not task_type-keyed — a Main-PM root is planning-typed, never code.
intents.name: submit_root

intents.description: Cell PM opens the cell→root PR and moves the cell task into the PR-review gate (awaiting_pr_review). The cell reviewer reviews the assembled diff; after pr_pass the same Cell PM completes it.
intents.name: submit_up

intents.description: Rebase your task's branch onto its current base THROUGH the gate (raw git is denied). Use when your branch has fallen behind its base — e.g. a sibling task's PR merged into the parent branch while you worked. Fetches origin, rebases head onto base, and force-pushes (with-lease). No DB state change. On conflicts the rebase is aborted and the conflicted files are returned — resolve by hand, commit, then sync_branch again.
intents.name: sync_branch

intents.description: List actionable tasks in your scope.
intents.name: triage

intents.description: List actionable tasks across all teams (Main PM only).
intents.name: triage_all

intents.description: PM unblocks a blocked task; restores pre-block state.
intents.name: unblock

intents.description: Voluntarily release a claim back to pending. The work-in-progress branch is preserved. A PR reviewer who claimed an external review (in_progress) or a gate review (awaiting_pr_review) and cannot finish releases the claim here rather than wedging the lane until the stale-claim reaper.
intents.name: unclaim
